applied_optimizations.name: conv_transpose_to_conv2d
applied_optimizations.description: Convert transposed conv2d to regular conv2d with flipped weights and padding=kernel_size-1. This hits a faster MIOpen kernel path. Achieved 5.16ms vs 5.29ms baseline (1.025x speedup, SCORE: 60.3)

attempted_but_failed.name: manual_triton_kernel
attempted_but_failed.reason: Complex 4D indexing for transposed conv is error-prone

attempted_but_failed.name: torch_compile_max_autotune
attempted_but_failed.reason: Significantly slower due to Triton kernel overhead

notes: Key insight: For stride=1, padding=0, dilation=1, transposed conv is equivalent to regular conv with flipped weights
Padding for equivalent conv2d: padding = kernel_size - 1
Regular conv2d hits a faster MIOpen kernel path than transposed conv
Final result: 5.16ms vs 5.29ms baseline = 1.025x speedup, SCORE: 60.3
